name: San Marino

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Tourist Entry (De Facto Schengen Visa-Free)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism and short visits to San Marino for nationals who are exempt from Schengen area visa requirements. San Marino has no independent border controls and entry is via Italy.
visa_types.notes: San Marino does not issue its own entry visas. Entry is governed de facto by Schengen Area rules since San Marino is entirely surrounded by Italy. Nationals who may enter the Schengen Area without a visa may enter San Marino freely. The San Marino MFA states that stays up to 30 days do not require a permit; stays beyond 30 days require a Stay Permit from the Gendarmerie - Foreigners' Office. The effective outer ceiling under the Schengen framework is 90 days in any 180-day period. No passport control at the San Marino border; no entry stamps are issued.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Tourism (Permesso di Soggiorno per Turismo)
visa_types.purpose: Formal stay permit for tourists wishing to remain in San Marino for more than 30 days and up to 12 months.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at the Gendarmerie - Foreigners' Office within the first 30 days of stay. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable. This is a temporary stay permit, not an entry visa. Exact fee and processing time not published on the official English page.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Work (Permesso di Soggiorno per Lavoro)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary stay permit for foreign nationals employed in San Marino. Required for stays exceeding 30 days for the purpose of work.
visa_types.notes: Maximum validity 12 months, renewable upon request. Application submitted to the Gendarmerie - Foreigners' Office. Specific documentation requirements and fee not detailed on the official English page.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Education (Permesso di Soggiorno per Studio)
visa_types.purpose: Special stay permit for foreign nationals pursuing education in San Marino.
visa_types.notes: Listed under 'special permits' category on the official MFA page, alongside sport, medical treatment, health assistance, rehabilitation, religion, humanitarian reasons, and international volunteering. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Family Reunification (Permesso di Soggiorno per Ricongiungimento Familiare)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary stay permit for spouses and children of foreign nationals legally resident in San Marino.
visa_types.notes: Available for spouse and children (family reunification) and cohabitation. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable. Application to the Gendarmerie - Foreigners' Office.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Working Holiday Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Special temporary stay permit for foreign nationals participating in a working holiday scheme in San Marino.
visa_types.notes: Listed explicitly under the 'special permits' category on the official MFA page. Eligibility conditions, eligible nationalities, and quotas not detailed on the official English page. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable.

visa_types.name: Stay Permit – Medical Treatment (Permesso di Soggiorno per Cure Mediche)
visa_types.purpose: Special stay permit for foreign nationals undergoing medical treatment, health assistance, or rehabilitation in San Marino.
visa_types.notes: Covers medical treatment, health assistance, and rehabilitation. Listed under the 'special permits' category on the official MFA page. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable.

visa_types.name: Pensioners' Atypical Residence (Residenza Atipica per Pensionati)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence for retirees from EU countries and Switzerland, based on pension income or movable assets, with a facilitated tax regime.
visa_types.notes: Limited to citizens from EU countries, Switzerland, and additional nations specified by a Congress of State Regulation. Minimum annual pension/passive income of €50,000 OR movable assets of at least €300,000 required. Facilitated taxation at 6% on pension income. Private health insurance required. No public sector employment; no state benefits. Converts to registered residence after 10 years. Path to citizenship is 30 years total continuous registered residence.

visa_types.name: Elective Residence Permit (Residenza Elettiva)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence for foreign nationals who make and maintain a qualifying property or financial investment in San Marino.
visa_types.notes: Under Law 94/2017, Article 19. Three investment options: (a) purchase of completed residential property for ≥€500,000; (b) purchase of unfinished building for ≥€350,000 plus completion costs (total capped at €500,000); (c) non-interest-bearing 10-year restricted deposit of ≥€600,000 in State bonds. Application fee €1,000; one-time tax of €10,000 (deposit option). Private health insurance required. No public sector employment; no state benefits. Converts to registered residence after 10 years. Family dependants can be added at €20,000 per person.

visa_types.name: Atypical Residence – Facilitated Tax Regime (Residenza Atipica)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence for foreign nationals generating income abroad who wish to establish residence in San Marino under a facilitated flat tax.
visa_types.notes: For foreign citizens generating income abroad who have not previously resided in San Marino for tax purposes. Substitute facilitated tax rate of 7% on foreign-source income, with a minimum of €10,000 and maximum of €100,000 annually. Private health insurance required. Cannot work in public sector or receive state benefits. Converts to registered residence after 10 years.

visa_types.name: Registered Residence for Foreign Nationals (Residenza per Stranieri)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term or permanent residence for foreign nationals seeking family reunification with San Marino citizens, establishing businesses, or holding management positions in San Marino companies.
visa_types.notes: Available for family reunification with San Marino citizens, business establishment, or management roles in local institutes or companies. This is the standard long-term registered residence status; atypical and elective residence types convert to registered residence after 10 years. Path to citizenship via naturalization is 30 years continuous registered residence (standard) or 15 years for spouses of San Marino citizens.

visa_types.name: Special Stay Permit – Sport, Religion, Humanitarian, Volunteering
visa_types.purpose: Special temporary stay permits for foreign nationals in San Marino for sport activities, religious purposes, humanitarian reasons, or international volunteering.
visa_types.notes: Grouped under 'special permits' on the official MFA page alongside education, sport, medical treatment, health assistance, rehabilitation, religion, humanitarian reasons, and international volunteering. Each sub-category likely has distinct documentation requirements not detailed on the official English page. Maximum validity 12 months, renewable.

visa_free.nationality: All Schengen Area visa-exempt nationals (de facto)
visa_free.notes: San Marino has no independent border controls and is only accessible via Italy (a Schengen member). Schengen entry rules apply de facto: all nationalities that may enter the Schengen Area without a visa may enter San Marino freely. No passport control at the border; no entry stamps issued. The official MFA page states stays exceeding 30 days require a permit from the Gendarmerie - Foreigners' Office; the Schengen 90/180-day rule is referenced by the UK government and travel sources as the effective outer limit for tourists. Visitors from non-Schengen states must obtain Schengen authorisation before departure.
San Marino maintains an entirely open border with Italy. Italian nationals have full freedom of movement under the bilateral friendship treaty between Italy and San Marino.

Bilateral agreement on abolition of tourist visa requirements. Signed April 19, 2012 (San Marino) and April 18, 2013 (Rabat); ratified October 23, 2013; in force October 28, 2013. Agreement title: 'Agreement between the Republic of San Marino and the Kingdom of Morocco on the abolition of tourist visa requirements.' Passport types covered not specified in the treaty summary page. Duration of 90 days is inferred from standard bilateral agreements; the official esteri.sm treaty page does not state a specific day count.

visa_policy.notes: San Marino does not issue independent entry visas. There is no San Marino-specific visa; entry is governed by Schengen Area rules since San Marino is entirely surrounded by Italy. Visitors from non-Schengen states must obtain Schengen authorisation before travelling. The official MFA page does not enumerate a country-by-country visa-free list; it directs travellers to Schengen member embassies. San Marino has signed bilateral visa abolition agreements with multiple countries (Argentina, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, China, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Finland, Hungary, Japan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Latvia, Lithuania, Morocco, Portugal, Romania, Russia, Slovenia, South Korea, United Kingdom) but these primarily affect San Marino passport holders travelling outbound; the official English MFA page does not publish a confirmed inbound visa-free list beyond the Schengen framework. Only the Morocco agreement was confirmed on an official esteri.sm URL. ETIAS (EU travel authorisation) remains expected in the last quarter of 2026 and is not yet operational as of June 2026; San Marino nationals are exempt from ETIAS.

cbi.notes: San Marino has no formal citizenship-by-investment programme. Citizenship is governed by Law No. 114 of 30 November 2000 and subsequent amendments (Laws 84/2004, 38/2016, Law 131/2021 amnesty, and the February 2026 amendment). The 2026 amendment (law approved approximately February 18, 2026) removed the prior requirement to renounce all other citizenships upon naturalization, fully legalizing dual/multiple citizenship for naturalization applicants. Naturalization residency requirements per secondary sources (Wikipedia): 20 consecutive years of registered and actual residence (standard), reduced to 10 years for spouses or civil partners of Sammarinese citizens; approved by the Grand and General Council. NOTE: the previous record stated 30 years (standard) / 15 years (spouse) based on the Law 114/2000 framework as of 2023; the 2026 residency figures of 20/10 years are confirmed by Wikipedia citing the amended law but could not be independently verified against an official machine-readable domain in this run — see gaps. dual_citizenship_allowed updated to true based on concordant secondary sources (Wikipedia, web search) and the existence of the consigliograndeegenerale.sm URL for the amending law (document was binary/PDF, not text-extractable). Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Marino_nationality_law and https://esteri.sm/pub1/EsteriSM/en/Servizi-per-utenza/Permessi-di-Soggiorno-e-Residenze.html

programs.name: Elective Residence Permit (Residenza Elettiva)
programs.notes: Under Law 94/2017, Article 19, foreign nationals may obtain elective residence via one of three options: (a) purchase of a completed residential building or portion for a price of not less than €500,000; (b) purchase of a raw/unfinished building for not less than €350,000, with additional completion/renovation costs not exceeding €150,000 (total cap effectively €500,000); or (c) a non-interest-bearing restricted 10-year deposit of not less than €600,000 in State of San Marino bonds or a specially created State fund. Application fee €1,000; one-time tax of €10,000 (deposit option). Private health insurance required. No public sector employment; no state benefits. Family extension available at €20,000 per additional dependent. After 10 years converts to registered residence (per official esteri.sm; one secondary source cites 5 years - esteri.sm is authoritative). Path to citizenship: 30 years total continuous registered residence. Investment amounts confirmed via startup.sm (official agency) referencing Law 94/2017.

programs.name: Atypical Residence – Facilitated Tax Regime (Residenza Atipica)
programs.notes: For foreign citizens generating income abroad who have not previously resided in San Marino for tax purposes. Subject to a substitute facilitated tax rate of 7% on foreign-source income, with a minimum of €10,000 and maximum of €100,000 annually. Private health insurance required. Cannot work in public sector or receive state benefits. After 10 years converts to registered residence. Source confirmed via esteri.sm and secondary sources referencing the same program parameters.

programs.name: Pensioners' Atypical Residence
programs.notes: Limited to citizens from EU countries, Switzerland, and countries specified by a specific Congress of State Regulation. Eligibility: minimum annual pension/passive income of €50,000 OR movable assets of at least €300,000. Facilitated taxation at 6% on pension income. Private health insurance required. No public sector employment; no state benefits. After 10 years converts to registered residence. Source: esteri.sm official page (types listed) and castellidisanmarino.com (amounts). The exact list of non-EU/Swiss eligible nations is set by Congress of State Regulation and not published on the English esteri.sm page.

programs.name: Automatic Residence for Investors (Entrepreneurial Project)
programs.notes: Under Delegated Decree 63/2014: entrepreneurs may obtain automatic residence if the business employs at least 5 full-time permanent staff on the startup job list (if >5, at least 50% must be San Marino residents). Property investment of €300,000 minimum (purchase or equivalent guarantee) required within 90 days of project approval; the property must serve as business headquarters or residence and carries a lien as wage guarantee. Priority sectors include technology, green economy, hospitality/tourism, entertainment, retail, arts/culture, healthcare/pharmaceuticals. Annual cap applies.

programs.name: Automatic Residence for Retail Trade
programs.notes: Requires minimum commercial space of 200 m² and either property purchase OR a multi-year rental with a €150,000 surety deposit. Minimum 2 employees from job placement lists. Maintenance period 10 years. Sourced from toccacelibronzetti.sm (a San Marino-registered accounting/fiscal firm) citing applicable legislation; not yet confirmed on a .gov or esteri.sm official domain URL.

programs.name: Residence for Company Founders (Startup Incentive / Residenza per Ragioni Economiche)
programs.notes: Under Delegated Decree 63/2014 and Law 115/2017: requires ≥51% ownership of a startup. Employment thresholds: 1 permanent employee from incentivized sectors OR 3 permanent employees from other sectors (or applicant is under 40 years old). Bank deposit guarantee required: €75,000 initially, increasing to €150,000 within 2 years; alternatively, property purchase with minimum value €150,000. Maximum outstanding debt: €20,000 (unless on deferred payment plan). Annual cap: 50 approvals per year (adjustable by decree).

programs.name: Business Purpose Residence (Residenza per Motivi di Business)
programs.notes: For shareholders holding ≥25% of company capital, sole administrators, or board chairmen. Eligible sectors: technology, green economy, arts/culture, development services, marketing, research, training, low emissions, health/pharmaceuticals. Requires private health insurance with minimum €30,000 annual coverage per person. Adequate housing documentation required. Duration: 12 months initial, renewable up to 5 years. No specific minimum investment amount stated; residency is linked to active company role rather than passive investment.

programs.name: High-Tech Startup Employee Special Permit
programs.eligibility: Legal representatives, partners, and employees of qualifying high-tech startups registered in San Marino
programs.notes: Duration: 1 year, renewable. Annual cap of 100 permits. Legal basis: Law 118/2010 and Law 115/2017. Holders are not entitled to free public healthcare. Governed by startup.sm, the official San Marino agency for economic development.

agreements: Open border / bilateral freedom of movement with Italy
De facto Schengen Area (no border checks with Italy; Schengen entry rules apply)

validation_notes: CORRECTED - max_stay_days for de facto Schengen visa-free entry: changed from 30 to 90. The official MFA page references a 30-day threshold triggering a stay-permit application requirement, but the applicable travel ceiling under de facto Schengen rules is 90 days in any 180-day period (confirmed by UK government official travel advice and general Schengen framework). The 30-day figure is an administrative trigger for a permit, not the visa-free entry limit.

CORRECTED - Morocco treaty notes: the treaty summary page confirms the agreement title as 'abolition of tourist visa requirements' but does not state specific stay days. The 90-day figure in the previous record was inferred; this inference is retained but flagged more clearly in the notes.

CORRECTED - Elective Residence: added the third real estate option (raw/unfinished building at ≥€350,000 plus up to €150,000 completion costs) which was omitted from the previous record. The min_amount now reflects the lower threshold of €350,000 rather than €500,000.

CORRECTED - Added 'Pensioners' Atypical Residence' as a distinct RBI program (previously omitted from the rbi.programs list; referenced only in gap notes). Minimum income €50,000/year OR €300,000 movable assets; 6% facilitated tax rate on pensions.

CORRECTED - Added 'Automatic Residence for Retail Trade' as a new program identified in updated sources (200 m² commercial space + €150,000 surety or property purchase + 2 employees). Marked source_official: false pending confirmation on an official domain.
CORRECTED - Residence for Company Founders: updated min_amount from null to €75,000 (bank deposit guarantee, increasing to €150,000 within 2 years). Previously the record showed null.

CORRECTED - Added 'Business Purpose Residence' (≥25% shareholding) as a distinct program separate from the Automatic Residence for Investors (≥5 employees + €300,000 property). These are two different programs.
CORRECTED - High-Tech Startup permit: legal basis updated to reference Law 118/2010 (confirmed via startup.sm) in addition to Law 115/2017.

CORRECTED - CBI notes: updated to reflect 30 years standard and 15 years for spouses (per sanmarinosite.com official secondary source), consistent with prior record. Added note on 2026 dual citizenship amendment claim (unverified on official domain).

UNCHANGED - conditional_access remains []. San Marino has no immigration checkpoints; no official San Marino-specific conditional access rules (e.g., held-credential, diplomatic-only) could be confirmed from official esteri.sm sources.

ADDED - visa_types field populated from official esteri.sm sources. San Marino does not issue standalone entry visas; all visa_types represent stay permits issued after arrival for stays beyond 30 days, or long-term residence categories. No tourist visa, transit visa, or e-visa exists as San Marino relies entirely on the Schengen framework for entry control.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27 - Full re-check against live official sources. esteri.sm/Informazioni-per-chi-visita-SMR.html confirmed: de facto Schengen entry rules, 30-day permit trigger, no nationality-specific enumeration. Morocco bilateral treaty page (esteri.sm/pub2/dettaglio_accordi) confirmed: signed 2012-2013, in force Oct 28 2013, title 'abolition of tourist visa requirements', no specific stay-day count stated. UK FCDO confirmed: British nationals visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period, consistent with Schengen framework. No new bilateral visa agreements found for 2025-2026 via WebSearch and esteri.sm sources. No 2026 dual citizenship amendment found on consigliograndeegenerale.sm legislative texts in English - the claimed amendment remains unconfirmed; dual_citizenship_allowed remains false. No e-visa, ETA, or VOA system exists for San Marino. No changes made to visa_policy, conditional_access, or visa_required entries - all entries re-confirmed against live official pages.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29 - Full re-check against live official sources. VISA POLICY UNCHANGED: esteri.sm/Informazioni-per-chi-visita-SMR.html re-fetched and re-confirmed: de facto Schengen entry rules, 30-day administrative permit trigger, no nationality-specific enumeration; entry requires Schengen authorisation for non-Schengen-exempt nationals. Morocco bilateral treaty page (esteri.sm/pub2/dettaglio_accordi) re-fetched and confirmed: signed Apr 2012/Apr 2013, in force Oct 28 2013, 'abolition of tourist visa requirements', no specific day count stated. UK FCDO re-fetched and confirmed: British nationals visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period. No new bilateral visa agreements for 2025-2026 found. No e-visa, ETA, or VOA system. No changes to visa_policy, conditional_access, or visa_required entries. CBI CORRECTED: dual_citizenship_allowed updated from false to true — the 2026 nationality law amendment (approved approximately February 18, 2026, per Wikipedia citing the amending law) removed the prior renunciation requirement; confirmed by concordant secondary sources and existence of the consigliograndeegenerale.sm PDF URL for the amendment (binary, not text-extractable). CBI notes updated to reflect the amendment and to flag the 20-year/10-year naturalization residency figures from Wikipedia as potentially reflecting the amended law (previously 30/15 from Law 114/2000 baseline); residency figures flagged in gaps pending official text-readable confirmation.
gaps: San Marino's official MFA website does not publish a complete enumerated country-by-country inbound visa-free list in English. Entry is governed by the Schengen framework rather than a standalone San Marino visa regime.

Bilateral visa abolition agreements with Argentina, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, China, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Finland, Hungary, Japan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Latvia, Lithuania, Portugal, Romania, Russia, Slovenia, South Korea, and United Kingdom are referenced in secondary sources. The esteri.sm treaty database requires a JavaScript-rendered search interface that could not be loaded directly; no individual treaty page was confirmed on an official domain for these countries.

Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements with Azerbaijan, Gambia, Moldova, Eswatini, Tunisia, Turkey, and Uganda are cited in Wikipedia. Not confirmed on any official domain (esteri.sm treaty database inaccessible); excluded from conditional_access per official-source-first policy.

Morocco treaty max_stay_days: the treaty summary page on esteri.sm does not state a specific day count. The 90-day figure in the record is inferred from standard bilateral practice; the full treaty PDF was not available for extraction.

2026 dual citizenship amendment: confirmed by multiple concordant secondary sources (Wikipedia, legal services web search results) that San Marino amended its nationality law in February 2026 to legalize dual citizenship by removing the renunciation requirement. The consigliograndeegenerale.sm URL for the amending law exists (https://www.consigliograndeegenerale.sm/on-line/documento17137723.html) but the document is a binary PDF, not text-extractable. dual_citizenship_allowed has been updated to true in this run. OUTSTANDING GAP: the precise naturalization residency period under the amended law (Wikipedia states 20 years standard, 10 years for spouses) has not been confirmed from an official text-readable source; the previous record was based on Law 114/2000 (30 years standard, 15 years for spouses). The CBI notes reflect the Wikipedia figures with a caveat; all RBI path_to_citizenship_years remain at 30 years pending official confirmation of the amended period.

Elective Residence conversion period: the official esteri.sm page states 10 years to conversion to registered residence; one secondary source (toccacelibronzetti.sm) cites 5 years. Retaining 10 years per official source.
Pensioners' Atypical Residence: the specific list of non-EU/Swiss eligible nations is determined by a Congress of State Regulation not published on the English esteri.sm page.

Automatic Residence for Retail Trade: confirmed via toccacelibronzetti.sm (San Marino fiscal studio citing relevant law) but not yet confirmed on an official government domain URL. Marked source_official: false.

ETIAS (EU travel authorisation) is expected to become operational in late 2026 but is not yet in force as of June 2026. When operational, Schengen-entry-required visitors will need ETIAS authorization to transit Italy en route to San Marino. San Marino nationals are explicitly exempt from ETIAS.
No official San Marino e-visa or ETA system exists.
Processing times for residence permit applications are not stated on the official English esteri.sm pages.
Exact fees for stay permits are not published on the official English esteri.sm pages.

The 'special permits' category on the official MFA page groups sport, medical, education, rehabilitation, religion, humanitarian, and working holiday into one umbrella; individual conditions per sub-category are not detailed in the official English content.
San Marino does not appear to have a formal digital_nomad or business visa separate from the existing residence permit categories.

visa_on_arrival.name: Algeria
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA only for S. Algeria cities (Timimoun, Ghardaia, Ilizi, Djanet, Tamanraset), max 30 days; visa at embassy otherwise

visa_on_arrival.name: Bahrain
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA up to 1 month or e-visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Bhutan
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA after pre-arranged tour operator authorization

visa_on_arrival.name: Egypt
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA 30 days or e-visa; Sinai resort airports visa-exempt up to 15 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Iran
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA at select airports (30 days) or e-visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Laos
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA at Luang Prabang, Pakse, Vientiane (30 days) or e-visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Madagascar
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA up to 90 days or e-visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Mozambique
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA at major airports (30 days) or e-visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Seychelles
visa_on_arrival.note: Visitor permit on arrival (up to 3 months) plus mandatory eTA before departure

visa_on_arrival.name: Syria
visa_on_arrival.note: VOA for countries without Syrian diplomatic representation

eta.name: United Kingdom
eta.note: ETA required; visa-free up to 6 months

outbound_official.notes: Data extracted from San Marino Ministry of Foreign Affairs (esteri.sm) country-by-country travel information pages at https://www.esteri.sm/pub1/EsteriSM/Servizi-per-utenza/Informazioni-per-chi-viaggia.html. Each destination was verified individually. Page does not carry a single update date; individual country pages have varying update dates (one noted as January 28, 2026). Countries requiring a full consular visa (not listed above) include: Afghanistan, Algeria (general), Angola (e-visa), Bolivia, Brunei, Chad, Central African Republic, Cuba (tourist card), Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Guyana, Iraq, Kiribati, Liberia, Libya, Mali, Marshall Islands, Myanmar, Nauru, Niger, North Korea, Paraguay, Republic of Congo, Sierra Leone, Sudan, Tonga, Turkmenistan, Yemen, and others. Latvia requires online registration via eta.gov.lv at least 48 hours before arrival (as of September 2025) but is otherwise visa-free. South Korea requires an e-arrival card before departure but is otherwise visa-free (30 days). Seychelles requires both an eTA before departure AND a visitor permit on arrival. Cape Verde requires EASE pre-registration before arrival but is visa-free for 30 days. Dominican Republic requires a tourist card on arrival ($10).
